notes: Pi streams a longer assistant reply across several JSONL flushes within ONE turn; the whole streamed turn must be a SINGLE contiguous working→ready arc with NO premature flicker to ready before the turn-end signal lands. Pi's turn boundary is defined ONLY by stopReason=stop (parser.go:78-83): every non-terminal assistant flush maps to `assistant` (mid-turn) and sustains working; only the final stopReason=stop assistant message maps to turn_done (working→ready). So partial/streamed writes never cause a spurious state flap. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE pi driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh tmux REPL), NOT headless `pi --print`: headless pi exits the instant it emits the final reply so the daemon's scanner can miss the terminal settle before process_exited (concern C6); the REPL keeps pi alive across one send + wait_turn so the settle is observed cleanly, then a single tmux teardown yields one clean process_exited. The prompt forces a LONGER reply (a 6-line haiku, one line at a time) so multiple flushes stream before stop. NOTE: a live CLI cannot be forced to emit a torn/partial JSONL line on demand — this cell asserts STATE STABILITY across streamed content (one working→ready arc, no intermediate ready flap), not a hand-crafted oversized/torn line (that is the separate oversized-transcript-line cell). C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (it bridges the gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript appears), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session (same shape as pi/session-start). turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher binds the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never enters working); every later phase pins to it via same_session_as. KEY ASSERTION: exactly ONE working→ready arc despite streaming — the streaming_window invariant 'no state_transition to ready' over the working span forbids any intermediate ready flap from a partial flush, and the no-waiting invariant forbids any waiting transition. HOW runs differ: exact haiku wording, token counts, sub-second timing, and the exact number of streamed flushes vary (model-output variance); the state topology (single sustained working span, no waiting, one process_exited, final ready) is stable. Single UUID rollout, one process_exited.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch

User prompt → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working). The streaming window opens here: state stays working across every partial flush — no flicker to ready before the turn-end signal arrives
Only the terminal stopReason=stop assistant message settles working→ready; despite multiple streamed flushes the turn is one contiguous arc and state never entered waiting
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the streamed turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
